description:
pyDREAM MonoSQ Legacy (MDScatter)

Purpose:
Legacy-compatible monodisperse hard-sphere SAXS template reconstructed from the historical MDScatter pyDREAM and Prefit workflow. Use this when reproducing older MDScatter notebook behavior is more important than the point-normalized likelihood convention used by newer SAXSShell templates.

Structure Factor:
Monodisperse hard-sphere structure factor evaluated with the Percus-Yevick expression used in the legacy notebooks and template_likelihood_monosq.py. eff_r controls the effective hard-sphere radius and vol_frac controls the packing term.

Form Factor:
The model uses a linear mixture of MD-derived component profiles. The component weights w0, w1, ..., wN are multiplied by the corresponding simulated SAXS traces, summed, and then modulated by S_HS(q).

Model Equation:
I_mix(q) = sum_i w_i I_i(q)
I_raw(q) = I_mix(q) S_HS(q; eff_r, vol_frac) + solv_w I_solv(q)
I_model(q) = scale I_raw(q) + offset

Legacy Convention:
Unlike the newer normalized MonoSQ templates, the DREAM likelihood returns the raw sum of Gaussian log-probabilities over fitted q-points. The Gaussian sigma is fixed at 1e-4, matching the notebook and template convention. The default scale is 1e-10 and fixed by default, matching the legacy pyDREAM hard-coded scale; Prefit can still expose the value for inspection or manual override.

Model Parameters:
w0, w1, ..., wN: Relative contribution of each MD-derived component profile.
solv_w: Solvent trace multiplier placed inside the global scale branch, with a broad legacy upper bound for notebook-scale solvent weights.
offset: Additive baseline term applied after global scaling.
eff_r: Effective hard-sphere radius used in the structure-factor calculation.
vol_frac: Hard-sphere volume fraction used by the Percus-Yevick structure factor.
scale: Multiplicative scale applied to the solute plus weighted-solvent model branch.
